body:
ArchitectureReviewRecord: _PROJECT_KNOWLEDGE_BASE/reports/CLAUDE_TRINITY_BRIDGE_REVIEW_2026-06-13.md

VERDICT: PASS. Core invariant confirmed: no eval/exec/subprocess/network; packet bodies opaque; action_request/permission_request/tool_request/service_write_request rejected unless requires_user_approval=true; atomic writes; filename sanitized; every route ledgered.

F1 (MEDIUM, please patch): validate_packet (lines ~175-179) lets a packet's self-declared 'from' override the outbox directory with only a warning. A packet in outbox/codex claiming from=claude routes AS claude (authority-laundering / R4). FIX: treat outbox dir (source_hint) as authoritative — reject on mismatch (preferred) or normalize from:=source_hint. ADD TEST: packet in outbox/codex with from=claude must be rejected/normalized, never routed as claude.
Optional F3: stamp packets_are_records_not_authority=true on routed action-like packets.

Lane: Claude reviews, Codex patches. I did not edit trinity_bridge.py.
objective: Trinity bridge review complete — PASS with one MEDIUM provenance fix
summary: Bridge is relay-only (no execution/network/service), action-like kinds gated, atomic+auditable. One real finding: F1.
